name: Mix
description: 4-channel DC-coupled mixer. Four attenuator knobs sum four inputs to a single output. Accepts audio or CV interchangeably. 10Vpp input range, 45mm deep.

inputs.name: Input 1
inputs.description: Signal input for channel 1. DC-coupled, accepts audio or CV.

inputs.name: Input 2
inputs.description: Signal input for channel 2. DC-coupled, accepts audio or CV.

inputs.name: Input 3
inputs.description: Signal input for channel 3. DC-coupled, accepts audio or CV.

inputs.name: Input 4
inputs.description: Signal input for channel 4. DC-coupled, accepts audio or CV.

outputs.name: Output
outputs.description: Summed output of all four channels.

behaviors.name: DC coupling
behaviors.description: All inputs and the output are DC-coupled, so CV, envelopes, gates, and pitch signals pass unchanged. Useful for summing offsets, mixing modulation sources, or combining multiple gate signals into one.

behaviors.name: Unity gain only
behaviors.trigger: all knobs fully CW with multiple inputs
behaviors.description: Knobs are pure attenuators — maximum gain is unity. Summing four full-level signals drives the output near +/-20V peak on CV or produces loud audio peaks. Back off individual knobs to keep the sum inside eurorack range.

behaviors.name: CV offset summing
behaviors.description: Because each input is DC-coupled with its own attenuator, Mix functions as a weighted offset adder. Great for combining a pitch sequence with an octave switch voltage, or summing LFO plus sequencer modulation on a single cutoff line.

patchIdeas.name: 4-voice audio submix
patchIdeas.patch: Four voice outs → Input 1-4 · balance with Level 1-4 · Output → main mixer · compact submix for drum or chord voice group that otherwise eats HP
patchIdeas.diagram:
Mix:

Drum 1:
Drum 2:
Drum 3:
Drum 4:

- Drum 1 (Out) -> Mix (Input 1)
- Drum 2 (Out) -> Mix (Input 2)
- Drum 3 (Out) -> Mix (Input 3)
- Drum 4 (Out) -> Mix (Input 4)
- Mix (Output) -> Main Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Modulation summer
patchIdeas.patch: LFO 1 → Input 1 · LFO 2 → Input 2 · S&H → Input 3 · envelope → Input 4 · Output → filter cutoff · combine four modulation sources with per-source amount on one cutoff — cheaper than a dedicated CV mixer
patchIdeas.diagram:
Mix:

LFO 1:
LFO 2:
S&H:
Envelope:
Filter:

- LFO 1 (Out) >> Mix (Input 1)
- LFO 2 (Out) >> Mix (Input 2)
- S&H (Out) >> Mix (Input 3)
- Envelope (Out) >> Mix (Input 4)
- Mix (Output) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Pitch + transpose summer
patchIdeas.patch: Sequencer 1V/oct → Input 1 (Level 1 full CW for accurate tracking) · octave switch (0/+1/+2V) → Input 2 · slow LFO (vibrato) → Input 3 · Output → VCO 1V/oct · transposable sequence with vibrato in a 2HP footprint
patchIdeas.diagram:
Mix:
* Level 1: full CW

Sequencer:
Octave Switch:
LFO:
VCO:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Mix (Input 1)
- Octave Switch (Out) p> Mix (Input 2)
- LFO (Sine) >> Mix (Input 3)
- Mix (Output) p> VCO (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Gate OR combiner
patchIdeas.patch: Three gate sources → Input 1-3 · Levels full CW · Output → gate destination · Mix sums the gates — any source firing opens the destination (analog OR) · downstream Schmitt/comparator cleans up the variable amplitude
patchIdeas.diagram:
Mix:
* Level 1-3: full CW

Gate 1:
Gate 2:
Gate 3:
Envelope:

- Gate 1 (Out) g> Mix (Input 1)
- Gate 2 (Out) g> Mix (Input 2)
- Gate 3 (Out) g> Mix (Input 3)
- Mix (Output) t> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Unity-gain crossfade simulator
patchIdeas.patch: Source A → Input 1 · Source B → Input 2 · manually pan between Levels 1 and 2 for simple mono crossfade · works as a quick no-VCA crossfade or A/B comparison utility in 2HP
patchIdeas.diagram:
Mix:

Source A:
Source B:

- Source A (Out) -> Mix (Input 1)
- Source B (Out) -> Mix (Input 2)
- Mix (Output) -> Output (In) // manual A/B balance via Level 1/2
